mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% as of 2011-08-01 reflects post-crisis labor slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or negative UNRATE changes do not produce significantly lower forward returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below its 3-month moving average, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth, triggering negative equity returns within 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprrice earnings.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes bond yields and redirects capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 226.268 as of 2011-08-01
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect (joint condition) shows no statistically significant improvement over single-series models (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month, indicating reduced manufacturing activity and supply-side weakness that compresses profit margins and signals recessionary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 52% of cases, or Rank IC in absolute value remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized weakness in consumer and residential demand that signals broad-based economic slowdown and negative equity repricing over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 604K and RSAFS at 389502M as of 2011-07-01 and 2011-08-01
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction model shows no significant improvement over univariate models (Vuong test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50K new jobs per month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market weakness or early recession
failure_condition: Triggered instances (PAYEMS growth < 50K) produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5%, creating expectations of future monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 226.268 as of 2011-08-01
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (t-stat < 1.96) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates quarter-over-quarter to below 1% annualized rate, signaling economic stagnation and reduced corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in the quarter following the data release.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.5B (Q1 2011 vintage) reflects post-crisis recovery plateau
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth < 1% produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window.
